sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.77 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Suppose that$G$is a periodic group containing an elementary abelian subgroup$R$of order 4. Must$G$be locally finite [r]{a)} if$C_G(R)$is finite? [r]{b)} if the centralizer of every involution of$R$in$G$is a Chernikov group? Remark of 1999:\/ P.\,V.\,Shumyatsky (Quart. J.~Math. Oxford (2), 49, no.\,196 (1998), 491--499) gave a positive answer to the question a) in the case where$G$is residually fin...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
